summary.intent: Integrate Mantine UI library into an Electron app and migrate all existing screens/components to use it, then progressively improve the UI with better table formatting, activity indicators, expandable rows, GitHub-style theming, and proper dark mode support.
summary.outcome: Mantine was fully integrated with PostCSS support, all screens and components were migrated to Mantine primitives, dark mode was implemented with persistent storage, and light/dark color issues were systematically fixed across SessionDetail, EventItem, ToolGroupItem, ToolUseItem, and MarkdownView using the `light-dark()` CSS function.

repo: Project is at /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer — Electron + electron-vite + React + React Router (MemoryRouter)
PostCSS config must be `postcss.config.cjs` (not `.js`) for the Vite setup; requires `postcss-preset-mantine` and `postcss-simple-vars`
Custom GitHub-inspired dark palette uses dark[0..9] = #e6edf3 → #000000 mapped into Mantine's theme
Activity status thresholds: <2min=green (active), <15min=orange (idle), else gray (inactive) — shared between SessionTree and SessionRow
Checkpoint data is fetched lazily via `/api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints` (existing endpoint); `createdAt` comes from `CheckpointSessionMetadata.createdAt` in Prisma schema
SessionRow must return a React fragment with two `Table.Tr` elements (main row + optional expanded row) to work inside Mantine Table

code.finding: AppShell.Header must span full viewport width to keep hamburger visible when sidebar is collapsed; placing toggle inside AppShell.Main causes it to hide with the sidebar
Use `light-dark(lightVal, darkVal)` CSS function for all background/border colors; static `var(--mantine-color-dark-7)` does not adapt to color scheme changes
All markdown renderer inline styles were hardcoded light colors; fenced code block background was hardcoded dark (#1e293b) which broke light mode — needed bidirectional light-dark() wrapping
CopyCell pattern: Table.Td with relative positioning, hover state adds paddingRight:28, absolutely-positioned ActionIcon at right edge for clipboard copy with brief ✓ confirmation

workflow: Mantine `-0` color variants (e.g. `var(--mantine-color-teal-0)`) are always the lightest shade regardless of color scheme — never use them directly; always wrap with `light-dark()`
When migrating to dark mode, audit every `backgroundColor: '#fff'`, `backgroundColor: '#f...'`, and `var(--mantine-color-X-0)` or `X-1` — these are the most common dark mode breakage points
`localStorageColorSchemeManager` with a named key persists color scheme preference across Electron app restarts
Build verification (`electron-vite build`) after each significant change catches type errors and import issues before runtime

friction: Dark mode required multiple rounds of fixes — each screen/component had independently hardcoded light colors that were only noticed when testing visually
AppShell hamburger placement required two iterations: first attempt put it in AppShell.Main (hidden when sidebar closed), second correctly moved it to AppShell.Header
Mantine's color system has a footgun: `-0` variants look like 'theme-aware' but are actually always light; not obvious from docs
SessionRow needed to be restructured to return fragments with multiple Table.Tr elements, which is a non-obvious constraint of Mantine's Table component
open_items: CheckpointDetail.tsx still uses `var(--mantine-color-teal-0)` and `var(--mantine-color-gray-0)` — dark mode fix was identified but not applied in this session
CheckpointMessageItem.tsx uses `var(--mantine-color-indigo-0)`, `#fff`, and orange border — dark mode fix identified but not applied
MarkdownView syntax highlighting uses a single hardcoded dark theme (`#1e293b`) for code blocks — a proper light-mode syntax theme would improve the light mode experience
The Mantine `Prism`/`CodeHighlight` component could replace the custom MarkdownView code rendering for better theme integration
